matchup: Oklahoma City Thunder at San Antonio Spurs
venue: Frost Bank Center, San Antonio, TX

model_snapshots.game_state_read: SAS needed 27 points in 11:06 while trailing by 13 with inefficient halfcourt offense and foul pressure on Castle/Keldon/Wembanyama.

okc.shai_gilgeous_alexander: 26 PTS, 12 AST, 12/12 FT; late-game stabilization through free throws and table-setting.
okc.jared_mccain: 24 PTS off bench; major spacing and shot-creation swing.
okc.jaylin_williams: 18 PTS, 5/6 3PT; key bench variance spike that pushed OKC scoring above baseline.
okc.hartenstein: 8 rebounds, 5 fouls; relevant to rebound redistribution and late frontcourt foul risk.

sas.wembanyama: 26 PTS but only 4 REB; scoring remained alive, rebounding muted.
sas.castle: 14 PTS, 7 AST, 5 PF; whistle volume helped but foul pressure reduced clean late-game ceiling.
sas.fox: 15 PTS, 7 REB, 6 AST; active but not enough downhill FT creation.
sas.keldon_johnson: 5 fouls in 11:40; reduced Spurs physical pressure and rotation flexibility.

phase_analysis.q1: SAS jumped to a 15-point lead behind Fox pace, Wembanyama early shot-making, and OKC turnovers. OKC bench threes reduced damage and kept game within 5 by quarter end.
phase_analysis.q2: Game flipped. OKC won 32-20 behind 63.2% shooting and 71.4% from three. SAS shot 31.2% and committed five turnovers for 12 OKC points.
phase_analysis.q3: Whistle-heavy quarter. OKC scored 37, SAS scored 33, but OKC maintained separation. SAS needed free throws to stay alive while OKC kept hitting enough perimeter shots.
phase_analysis.q4: OKC closed 28-24. SAS reached 108, falling short of the 110.5 live team total. Late bench points moved OKC to 123 and full-game total to 231.

positions: SAS over 110.5 TT NO avg -133: WIN

successful_reads: Halftime SAS team total under edge was real and survived fourth-quarter foul risk.
OKC team total over 108.5 pre/live position was correctly identified as structurally strong.
SAS offensive output depended too heavily on whistle and difficult halfcourt looks.
OKC bench shooting depth created ceiling pressure on OKC scoring lanes.
miss_or_caution_zones: Full-game under would have been fragile due to OKC bench and late reserve scoring.
SAS under survived but only by 2.5 points; late garbage-time threes remained a real tail risk.
Need better late-game reserve-scoring volatility detection when favorite lead is 14+ inside final two minutes.
